summary: Kenneth documents his week trading covered calls across 6 stocks with $64,881 deployed, generating $2,647 in net premium (4% return). Five positions were assigned at expiration while HIMS declined, leaving $23,420 tied up—illustrating both the income consistency and capital lock-up risks of the covered call wheel strategy.
takeaways: Target stocks with 100-share minimum positions (1 contract = 100 shares)
Accept assignment as normal outcome; capital gets returned and redeployed
Stock appreciation beyond strike price means missed upside but consistent premium income—'the goal isn't to make all of the money, just some of it'
Multiple positions per week (5-6 stocks) provides diversification and consistent income vs. timing single home runs
Recognize downside: capital can be tied up for extended periods if shares don't clear (HIMS locked $23,420 after dropping from $58 to $52)
Weekly format allows flexibility to adjust subsequent week selling if mid-week stock recovery occurs
Frame strategy as generating predictable passive income where 'you know what you're going to make ahead of time' vs. day/swing trading

strategy_candidates.change: Establish hard rule: if assigned stock drops >10% intra-week (like HIMS: $58→$52), immediately plan covered call sale for further-out expiration (10-21 DTE) rather than waiting for Monday to assess premium decay
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth explicitly noted HIMS Friday drop reduced Monday covered call premium outlook; proactive further-out strikes on dips would maintain income targeting while managing gap-down assignment risk

strategy_candidates.change: Track and report weekly premium-to-capital-deployed ratio as leading metric (currently 4.1% weekly ≈ $1,250/mo on $50k desk); flag when ratio drops below 3.5% to trigger position concentration review
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth achieved $2,647 on $64,881 deployed (4%); establishing thresholds ensures consistency with Brian's $1,250/mo target and flags when stock selection or strike selection needs adjustment

title: Part 17: 9/29-10/3: I made $2600 THIS WEEK. Here's How
